days.label: Friday
days.text: Friday night has serious range. The big one is "Weird Al" Yankovic ($69.50, kids 12 and under free) on the Midway Lawn at the Champlain Valley Expo in Essex Junction at 7:30, with Puddles Pity Party opening, an outdoor evening of accordion mischief and sad clown crooning that should be something to behold. Comedy fans staying downtown can catch Jordan Jensen at the Flynn at 7:30, fresh off her Netflix special "Take Me With You". Music people have choices all over the map, with Seven Daysies winning cover band Sticks and Stones playing the Old Post from 7 to 10, songwriter Jeffrey Foucault doing an early all ages show in the Light Club Lamp Shop room at Radio Bean at 7, and the Zach Nugent Duo bringing free Grateful Dead energy to the Skinny Pancake patio at 8 for their Dancing in the Streets series. Theater folks should note Steal Fire's "No Exit" at Off Center, a new multimedia staging of the Sartre classic at 7:30 tonight with a French existentialism talkback, plus 2 PM matinees Saturday and Sunday.

days.label: Saturday
days.text: Saturday belongs to the Old North End. The Ramble takes over the neighborhood from 8 in the morning to 10 at night with over 50 DIY pop-ups, including homemade waffle gardens on Manhattan Drive, a build-your-own bouquet bar at Burly Blooms, and driveway chess with "janky bleachers" on Oak Street. Neighbors will be out all day for front-porch art galleries, sidewalk lemonade stands, street-wide yard sales, the 4:15 PM community bike ride, and the evening Ramble Round Up beer garden. So be sure to come out and enjoy Burlington’s Old North End. The ONE World Market folds right into it from 11 to 3 on Allen Street with dozens of local vendors. Downtown, the BTV Market sets up outside City Hall from 11 to 3, DJ CRWD CNTL spins Splash Dance by the City Hall Park fountain from 1 to 3, and the Burlington Farmers Market does its 70 vendor thing on Pine Street from 9 to 2. Our own BTown Coffee Club [MEETUP] meets Saturday at 10 at Zero Gravity, big table on the right, newcomers always welcome, and then the crew heads to Paddle at Oakledge Beach [MEETUP] at 11 for a couple easygoing hours of kayaking and paddleboarding on the lake. Bound to be a great morning so come say hi.

days.label: Saturday
days.text: Saturday afternoon and evening keep rolling. Skaters can escape the heat at the Free Family Skate at Cairns Arena from 1:30 to 3 with $5 rentals, the Greek Mini-Festival brings souvlaki, spanakopita, and baklava to the Dormition Greek Orthodox Church lawn from 4 to 8, and the free community Summer BBQ on North Ave serves up burgers, hot dogs, live music, and yard games from 4 to 7. Kids in the Hall legend Kevin McDonald does two different shows at Vermont Comedy Club, his comic rock opera "Superstar" at 7 ($25) and the improvisers shoving him onstage for "Forced to Improvise" at 8:30 ($15) . Punk fans get Sub Pop provocateurs Downtown Boys at Foam Brewers at 8:30, Colchester gets high energy covers from Magnetic Horse at the Spanked Puppy at 7, tabletop people can hit the Board Game Brunch [MEETUP] at Old Town Cafe & Comics in Williston at 9, and anyone curious about basics can take the free 45 minute community self defense class at Oom Yung Doe on North Ave at 6.

days.label: Sunday
days.text: Sunday is a gem. Winooski crowns its Best Pooch in the Noosk at the Winooski Farmers Market , both running 10 to 2 at Winooski Falls Way, and every dog in the Onion City is invited to compete for titles like Cutest. Our group's Centennial Hills Hike and Winooski Circle [MEETUP] meets at Schmanska Park at 11 for a loop through Centennial Woods followed by an optional beer at the Winooski Circle, dogs welcome, and with a crowd this size you will make a friend or five. Craft types can build pebble art under the pavilion at Sam Mazza's in Colchester from 10 to 12 with proceeds supporting Camp Ta-Kum-Ta, brunch people have the Blueberry Jam Brunch at the Swaying Daisies cafe at Isham Family Farm in Williston from 11 to 3, and the morning starts gently with the string ensemble Skylark playing Sunday Classical in the BCA Center gallery on Church Street at 10. The Safety Team runs a 2.5 hour Empowerment Self-Defense Workshop at the South Burlington Community Room at 10, taught by women for women with scholarships available. Later on, Déjà Nous plays jazz and swing at Shelburne Vineyard at 1 for free, Womxn's pick-up soccer [MEETUP] kicks off at Starr Farm Park at 5, and the weekend closes big with Shakey Graves and Dope Lemon ($54.50, kids 12 and under free) at Ben & Jerry's Concerts on the Green at Shelburne Museum, music at 6, or a sunset hip hop dance cruise aboard the Spirit of Ethan Allen at 6:30 if you would rather groove on the water.
